---
# Rmarkdown header. it use Use the YAML syntax. 
# Just copy the ---YAML block --- and change title, subtyle, author and date
title: "Template" 
subtitle: "People Analytics"
author: "Jesús García Romanos"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: hide

# store the output in the subdirectory reports
knit: (function(inputFile, encoding) { 
      rmarkdown::render(inputFile,
                        encoding=encoding, 
                        output_dir=here::here("reports")) }) 
---

# Setup

Within project directory, if they do not exists, create the following directories.

- **data** to store the data created. R native dataformat is rds.\
- **reports** to store the HTML output files created when knitting.

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
#this chunk is hidden from the notebook output by adding ,include=FALSE to the chunk name.

library(tidyverse) 

library(here)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = here::here())

```

# First Title

```{r}

```
